mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 3.5% (CPIAUCSL) signals central bank inaction, leading to higher real interest rates and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite inflationary signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in FEDFUNDS and declines in CPIAUCSL signal a tightening cycle with disinflation, creating uncertainty that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises > 0.5% and CPIAUCSL falls > 0.3% in 1 month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite conflicting signals, or interaction effect size < 0.1 in rolling regressions.

mechanism_narrative: A tight labor market (UNRATE < 3.5%) drives wage inflation, prompting aggressive monetary policy tightening that reduces S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite low unemployment, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) indicates strong supply-side capacity utilization, creating deflationary pressures that drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 60% of instances with rising INDPRO, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating housing starts (HOUST) signal robust demand for shelter, increasing corporate revenues and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of instances with rising HOUST, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in PAYEMS and declines in UNRATE indicate a tightening labor market, increasing wage pressures that drive negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises > 100k and UNRATE falls > 0.2pp in 1 month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite labor market strength, or interaction effect size < 0.1 in rolling regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer credit (RSAFS) indicates heightened household demand, increasing corporate revenues and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of instances with rising RSAFS, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
